name: feedback_project_scoped_over_global
purpose: Enforce project-scoped config targets by default; never modify global (~/.claude/) configs without explicit user approval.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Prefer project-scoped config over global changes
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: When configuring tooling (statusline, settings, env), scope changes to the project by default. Don't modify global configs without explicit approval.

notes: No originSessionId in frontmatter. Triggered by statusline incident during JASON-OS bootstrap 2026-04-15 — agent replaced global statusline. Why/How-to-apply format. Highly portable: the rule is universal to any Claude Code project. Missing from canonical-memory — strong gap candidate. SoNash likely has analogous content if statusline was first built there.

name: feedback_scope_drift_deep_research
purpose: Prevent deep-research from being scoped onto a sub-component instead of the user's actual goal; research topic must map directly to stated objective.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Deep-research can hijack a project's identity when scoped on infrastructure instead of the actual goal. A full session was lost to this.

notes: No originSessionId. Body cites concrete incident: 'Intelligence Graph Data Layer' drift swallowed a 'unify four skills' initiative. Four numbered rules. Highly portable — applies to any deep-research invocation. Missing from canonical-memory — strong gap candidate.

name: feedback_skills_in_plans_are_tool_calls
purpose: Slash-commands in PLAN.md or plan documents are Skill tool invocations, not manual instructions to execute by hand.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Skill references inside plan documents are Skill tool calls, not prose
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: When a PLAN.md or other plan document mentions a slash command like /analyze, that is a Skill tool invocation — never execute the phases manually

notes: No originSessionId. Longest body of the batch — extensive because the cognitive failure mode is subtle (inside 'plan execution' frame, slash-commands stop reading as tool calls). Includes a code block example. Why/How-to-apply with corollary rule. Portable — fundamental to JASON-OS skill system. Missing from canonical-memory.

name: feedback_sonarcloud_mark_as_safe
purpose: After addressing any SonarCloud security hotspot, always explicitly prompt user to mark it in the SonarCloud UI — code change alone does not clear the To-Review queue.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Always surface when a SonarCloud security hotspot needs the user to click Mark-as-Safe/Fixed in the UI

notes: Has originSessionId (8e667a17). External ref to JASON-OS-specific SonarCloud URL — that URL is project-specific but the rule is portable. Triggered 2026-04-17 after first SonarCloud baseline pass. Contains user-action pattern (emoji flag). Missing from canonical-memory.

name: feedback_statusline_rebuild_safety
purpose: Never overwrite running statusline exe in-place; bump versioned filename and update shim pointer; git add shim to force Claude Code file-watcher re-pickup.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Claude Code holds a handle on the running statusline exe — never overwrite in-place; bump the versioned filename + update the shim pointer

notes: Has originSessionId (f6ce3805 — same session as project_sync_mechanism_principles; both from 2026-04-18). Contains JASON-OS-specific details (go.mod pinned go 1.26, build.sh paths) but the core rule (version-bump, shim pattern, git-add for watcher) is portable. Hard-rules section with 6 numbered steps. Newer memory (2026-04-18). Missing from canonical-memory.

name: feedback_testing_with_writes
purpose: Every code change must have its own tests written (unit, integration, deletion, regression) — not just existing tests run.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Every code change needs tests written for it, not just existing tests run — multi-level testing is mandatory, not optional

notes: Has originSessionId (142f84e3). Triggered by Session #286 where runReconcileCommits(), generateReviewId(), check-review-record.js hook were written with zero tests. Four numbered test levels. Why/Apply format. Portable — universal to any code-writing project. Missing from canonical-memory.

name: feedback_todo_graduation
purpose: Plan deferrals in Post-Foundation sections migrate to /todo backlog once /todo is operational; deferred items must not remain in prose only.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Todo-graduation — deferrals migrate to /todo once operational
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Plan Post-Foundation Deferrals sections are temporary homes for deferred items. As soon as /todo works, those items migrate into /todo as tracked backlog — deferred never = forgotten

notes: Has originSessionId (dbea5544 — same as reference_pr_review_integrations; both from same earlier session). References feedback_never_defer_without_approval.md by name — cross-memory dependency. JASON-OS-specific trigger condition cited (.planning/jason-os-mvp/PLAN.md, D9 Layer 0 trigger) but the principle is portable. Missing from canonical-memory.

name: feedback_user_action_steps
purpose: When a plan step requires user action (installs, auth, credentials), stop and prompt the user — do not document and move on.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_name: Never skip user-action steps in plans
existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: When a plan has steps requiring user action (installs, auth, credentials), PROMPT the user to do it — don't just document it and move on

notes: No originSessionId. Triggered by Gemini CLI install step that was documented but never actually prompted — feature was dormant. Four bullet rules. Portable — universal execution discipline. Missing from canonical-memory.

name: feedback_workflow_chain
purpose: The user's canonical workflow chain is brainstorm → deep-research → deep-plan → execute; never substitute superpowers skills at any step.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: User's workflow is brainstorm -> deep-research -> deep-plan -> execute. Never substitute superpowers skills for this chain.

notes: No originSessionId. Triggered by superpowers:writing-plans intercepting the chain and writing to docs/superpowers/plans/ instead of .claude/plans/. Concise body (no numbered list, narrative). Portable — the chain is the user's meta-workflow, applies across all projects. Missing from canonical-memory.

name: feedback_worktree_guidance
purpose: Worktrees for research/planning (read-heavy); branches for implementation. Built-in EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree tools, not skills/agents. Key limitations documented.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: When to use worktrees vs branches, built-in tools (EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree), known limitations, .worktreeinclude for file sync

notes: No originSessionId. Lists 5 key limitations including skills/agents/rules not being copied to worktrees, state files not syncing, and WorktreeCreate/Remove hooks not firing for git repos. Portable — applies to any Claude Code project. Missing from canonical-memory.

name: feedback_write_rejection_hard_stop
purpose: Write/Edit rejection by a hook = hard stop; retry immediately via Bash/Python; never proceed to next phase with missing artifacts.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: When Write tool is rejected by a hook, immediately retry via Bash/Python. Never proceed to next phase with missing artifacts.

notes: No originSessionId. Triggered by 4 analysis artifacts rejected by security hook (prose discussing security patterns triggered false positives). Three-step recovery protocol: check result, retry via python3 heredoc, block phase advance. Portable — applies to any project with hooks. Missing from canonical-memory.

name: project_cross_locale_config
purpose: Defines which state files sync via git (cumulative, non-reconstructable) vs stay local (session-scoped, self-healing) between home and work locales. Classification framework for new state files.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Which state files sync via git vs stay local between home and work computers. CL-verified 2026-03-22.

notes: Type=project. Not portable — contains JASON-OS-specific file names (consolidation.json, velocity-log.jsonl, health-score-log.jsonl, hook-warnings-log.jsonl, etc.) and locale paths (C:/Users/jason, C:/Users/jbell). The PATTERN (cumulative+non-reconstructable = git-tracked; session-scoped = local) is portable as a methodology, but the specific file classifications are project-specific. SoNash will have analogous file but with different filenames. Missing from canonical-memory — correct: project-scoped memories should not be in canonical.

name: project_jason_os
purpose: Full project status tracker for JASON-OS: origin, repo URL, completed layers, open items, direction, anti-goals.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Portable Claude Code OS. Bootstrap + MVP deep-research + Foundation deep-plan + Foundation EXECUTION COMPLETE. D19 gate CLOSED (partial Layer 4 only). Next: Step 6 /brainstorm sync-mechanism.

notes: Type=project. Not portable — entirely JASON-OS-specific: repo URL, session history, layer status, D19 gate, T-numbers, local paths, PR numbers. The largest and most-updated memory in the batch; description is updated frequently to reflect current status. Correctly absent from canonical. SoNash will have an analogous project_sonash.md. The 'Direction: Template → Platform' section contains strategic intent that is JASON-OS-specific. Memory links in project_sync_mechanism_principles reference this file by name.

name: project_sync_mechanism_principles
purpose: Load-bearing design principles for the sync-mechanism project: Approach A (scan first), overkill agents, port-over verification safety net, symmetric peer architecture, JASON-OS as test case, multi-phase discipline.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Load-bearing design principles for the sync-mechanism project (JASON-OS ↔ SoNash bidirectional sync). Scope-only-of-repo, overkill-agents default, port-over as safety net.

notes: Has originSessionId (f6ce3805 — same session as feedback_statusline_rebuild_safety; 2026-04-18, very recent). Type=project. Not portable in the sense that these principles are specific to THIS project's design. However, unique: it explicitly names memory_links (5 other memories by filename) — the richest cross-reference structure of any file in this batch. This cross-linking convention is novel and worth surfacing for schema design. 6 numbered design principles. The symmetric architecture principle (Principle 4) corrects a prior 'workshop-and-neighbor' error — documents a design reversal mid-project.

name: reference_pr_review_integrations
purpose: User's preferred PR-review integration set: Qodo + SonarCloud + Gemini. CodeRabbit explicitly excluded (noise/overlap). When to consult section included.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: User's preferred PR-review integration set = Qodo + SonarCloud (+ Gemini if CLI-based). CodeRabbit removed from SoNash due to overlapping reviews and noise.

notes: Has originSessionId (dbea5544). Type=reference. Scope is user-level preference (applies across all projects). Contains 'When to consult this memory' section — the only file in this batch with that navigation hint. Three-section structure: In use, Excluded, When to consult. Portable — PR-review tooling preference is project-agnostic. Missing from canonical-memory — gap. SoNash project-memory likely has the same content (preferences are universal).

name: t3_convergence_loops
purpose: Convergence-loop tenet — when/why discipline is required for multi-claim work; architecture of one skill with many callers; advanced patterns pointer.

existing_metadata.frontmatter_description: Convergence-loop tenet (when/why) plus /convergence-loop skill (how) — required discipline for multi-claim work.

notes: Unusual t3_ prefix — appears to be a tenet numbering convention (T3 = Tenet #3). Has 'status: active' field in frontmatter beyond the standard name/description/type — this extended frontmatter schema is notable. Type=reference, not feedback. No originSessionId. Body is concise: tenet summary, architecture note, high/medium value callers, advanced patterns pointer. The tenet/reference distinction vs feedback: tenets are principles that don't arise from a specific corrective incident — they're design philosophy. The t3_ prefix suggests a numbered tenet taxonomy that may have t1_, t2_, t4_ siblings not yet written. Missing from canonical-memory — gap. Note: feedback_convergence_loops_mandatory IS in canonical but is the 'behavioral feedback' version; t3_convergence_loops is the 'why/when design tenet' version — they serve different purposes.
